note: Hard-negative pairwise benchmark. v0 showed the fielded ranker at parity on an easy corpus where even a perfect ranker could not clear the +15% NDCG gate. This retires that gate and asks the sharper question: for a query, does the ranker place the positive asset ABOVE a deliberately confusable same-family negative? The baseline here is STRUCTURED (field-aware term overlap with per-field weights) rather than flat substring, so the fielded ranker must earn its complexity against a fair competitor. Triplets are generated by the runner: for each query, positive vs each same-family sibling that is not the query's own acceptable set. Decision rule (from the design consult): the fielded ranker must beat the structured baseline by >= 10 percentage points in hard-negative pairwise accuracy, or it leaves the production path and only the compaction ladder remains. Confusion families are the six clusters below plus the six new confusable siblings.

draft.title: Pending decisions list
draft.summary: A list of proposals still waiting for a decision, so a reviewer can triage which to open.

intent.userJob: triage a list of pending proposals and choose which one to open next

draft.title: Version conflict chooser
draft.summary: Presents two conflicting edits of the same artifact and asks which one becomes canonical.

intent.userJob: resolve a conflict between two competing edits by choosing which becomes canonical

draft.title: Sync progress bar
draft.summary: Shows how far an in-flight data sync has advanced and how much remains.

intent.userJob: show how far a running data sync has progressed and how much is left

draft.title: Time series line
draft.summary: Plots a single metric over time to show its trend across a period.

intent.userJob: plot a metric over time to show how it trended across a period

draft.title: Feature tour
draft.summary: A stepped tour that points out the main features to a brand-new user.

intent.userJob: walk a brand-new user through the main features with a stepped tour

draft.title: Slide grid
draft.summary: A grid of slide thumbnails for jumping directly to any slide in a deck.

intent.userJob: jump directly to any slide in a deck from a grid of thumbnails
